title: agropyron repens subsp. glaucum
intro: Agropyron repens subsp. glaucum, also known as couch grass or quack grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Agropyron repens, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Agropyron repens subsp. glaucum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Agropyron repens subsp. glaucum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine for its expectorant and diuretic properties.
